[TICKET_SUMMARY] GROMACS - mpirun question

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
Dear CSD3 Team,  
I would like to ask for advice on using GROMACS. When I run the "mdrun" executable  with mpirun across multiple nodes I get a large numbers of the following error mesage: 
[qelr_create_qp:683]create qp: failed on ibv_cmd_create_qp with 22  
Other GROMACS executables, which I run only on a single core do not make the same error. I assume this reflects some openMPI problem and I would like to ask whehter this has been noticed by others, and whehter there is a way of fixing this.  
I used the following job script: 
...
#SBATCH --partition skylake
#SBATCH --nodes 2
#SBATCH --ntasks 64
#SBATCH --time 36:00:00
#SBATCH --mem=16000
module purge
module load rhel7/default-peta4 gromacs/2019.3
MDRUN=mdrun_$SLURM_JOB_PARTITION
...
mpirun -np 64 $MDRUN -v -deffnm em
... 
Best wishes,  
Gyorgy Abrusan

#!#############################################################
#!#### Modify the options in this section as appropriate ######
#!#############################################################
#! sbatch directives begin here ###############################
#! Name of the job:
#SBATCH -J smolvla_mikasa
#! Which project should be charged (NB Wilkes2 projects end in '-GPU'):
#SBATCH -A T2-CS178-GPU
#! How many whole nodes should be allocated?
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#! How many (MPI) tasks will there be in total?
#! Note probably this should not exceed the total number of GPUs in use.
#SBATCH --ntasks=1
#! Specify the number of GPUs per node (between 1 and 4; must be 4 if nodes>1).
#! Note that the job submission script will enforce no more than 32 cpus per GPU.
#SBATCH --gres=gpu:1
#! How much wallclock time will be required?
#SBATCH --time=36:00:00
#! What types of email messages do you wish to receive?
#SBATCH --mail-type=ALL
#! Uncomment this to prevent the job from being requeued (e.g. if
#! interrupted by node failure or system downtime):
#SBATCH --no-requeue
#! Do not change:
#SBATCH -p ampere
#! sbatch directives end here (put any additional directives above this line)
#! Notes:
#! Charging is determined by GPU number*walltime.
#! Number of nodes and tasks per node allocated by SLURM (do not change):
numnodes=$SLURM_JOB_NUM_NODES
numtasks=$SLURM_NTASKS
mpi_tasks_per_node=$(echo "$SLURM_TASKS_PER_NODE" | sed -e 's/^([0-9][0-9]).$/\1/')
#! ############################################################
#! Modify the settings below to specify the application's environment, location
#! and launch method:
#! Optionally modify the environment seen by the application
#! (note that SLURM reproduces the environment at submission irrespective of ~/.bashrc):
. /etc/profile.d/modules.sh # Leave this line (enables the module command)
module purge # Removes all modules still loaded
module load rhel8/default-amp # REQUIRED - loads the basic environment
#! Insert additional module load commands after this line if needed:
#! Full path to application executable:
nvidia-smi
application="./convert.sh"
#! Run options for the application:
options=""
#! Work directory (i.e. where the job will run):
workdir="$SLURM_SUBMIT_DIR" # The value of SLURM_SUBMIT_DIR sets workdir to the directory
1. in which sbatch is run.
#! Are you using OpenMP (NB this is unrelated to OpenMPI)? If so increase this
#! safe value to no more than 128:
export OMP_NUM_THREADS=1
#! Number of MPI tasks to be started by the application per node and in total (do not change):
np=$[${numnodes}*${mpi_tasks_per_node}]
#! Choose this for a pure shared-memory OpenMP parallel program on a single node:
#! (OMP_NUM_THREADS threads will be created):
CMD="$application $options"
#! Choose this for a MPI code using OpenMPI:
#CMD="mpirun -npernode $mpi_tasks_per_node -np $np $application $options"
###############################################################
1. 1. 1. You should not have to change anything below this line ####
###############################################################
cd $workdir
echo -e "Changed directory to `pwd`.\n"
JOBID=$SLURM_JOB_ID
echo -e "JobID: $JOBID\n======"
echo "Time: `date`"
echo "Running on master node: `hostname`"
echo "Current directory: `pwd`"
if [ "$SLURM_JOB_NODELIST" ]; then
#! Create a machine file:
export NODEFILE=`generate_pbs_nodefile`
cat $NODEFILE | uniq > archive_logs/machine.file.$JOBID
echo -e "\nNodes allocated:\n================"
echo `cat archive_logs/machine.file.$JOBID | sed -e 's/\..*$//g'`
fi
echo -e "\nnumtasks=$numtasks, numnodes=$numnodes, mpi_tasks_per_node=$mpi_tasks_per_node (OMP_NUM_THREADS=$OMP_NUM_THREADS)"
echo -e "\nExecuting command:\n==================\n$CMD\n"
eval $CMD

CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / RDS / Quota Diagnosis
Technical Action Required: No (user action required to reduce file count)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium (job failing due to quota)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User reports `Disk quota exceeded` errors while writing files to an RDS project, despite not obviously expecting the project to be full. On RDS, this can happen either because the space quota is exhausted or because the inode/file-count quota has been reached. From the initial report alone, inode exhaustion is a strong possibility, especially given the shell errors creating temporary files, but `quota` output is needed to confirm whether the problem is space usage or file count.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1) Confirm which quota has been exhausted
   Ask the user to run:
     quota
   and check the relevant RDS project entry.
   - If the GB column is at or above the limit, this is a space-quota problem.
   - If the files column is at the limit or marked with an asterisk (*), this is an inode/file-count problem.

2) Explain the distinction between storage quota and inode quota
   - Storage quota = total disk space (GB/TB)
   - Inode quota = total number of files
   - Hitting either limit blocks new file creation

3) Provide remediation options

   Option A — Reduce file count (recommended if inode limit reached)
   - Delete unnecessary small files
   - Consolidate many small files into archives (e.g. tar)
   - Combine image files into fewer container formats if possible

   Example:
     tar -czf archive_episodes_12000_12100.tar.gz episode_0120*

   Option B — Increase storage allocation
   - Inode quota increases only when total RDS storage allocation increases.
   - This requires purchasing additional storage capacity.
   - Refer to RDS quota documentation.

4) Clarify policy
   - Inode quotas are fixed per TiB.
   - They cannot be increased independently of storage allocation.

5) Close once the user understands which limit was hit and how to proceed.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Subject: Re: Quota exceeded on RDS project

Hello Liyou,

The `Disk quota exceeded` message can be caused either by running out of space or by reaching the inode/file-count limit on the RDS project.

Please run:

  quota

and send us the output for the affected RDS project. If the limit has been hit in the `files` column rather than the GB column, that would indicate an inode quota issue rather than lack of storage space.

If it is an inode limit, the usual remedies are to remove or consolidate many small files or to increase the total storage allocation.

Best wishes,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not imply a filesystem fault without first confirming whether space or inode quota has been reached.
- Do not suggest modifying system quota settings directly.
- Inode quotas are proportional to purchased storage and are not adjustable independently.
- Avoid recommending unsafe bulk deletion commands.

I've been having trouble with getting jobs to complete. I've returned from a break, but all simulations I set up seem to have failed with a few different exit codes; 1, 255, 38 mostly.  
I'm using Castep and its magres module to calculate NMR parameters on very similar systems. As a results, most input files are identical to previously successful jobs.   
Exit codes of 1 and 255 have a line similar to this in the log. 
> slurmstepd: error: task_p_post_term: rmdir(/sys/fs/cgroup/cpuset/slurm46024470/slurm46024470.4294967294_0) failed Device or resource busy
>  
 
Exit codes of 38 have this in the log. 
 
>  forrtl: Disk quota exceeded 
> forrtl: severe (38): error during write, unit 10, file /rds/user/djm254/hpc-work/KCl/3xKCl/magres/800K/1000/KCl.$ 
> Image              PC                Routine            Line        Source 
> castep.mpi         0000000007FDD363  Unknown               Unknown  Unknown 
> castep.mpi         000000000804076F  Unknown               Unknown  Unknown 
> castep.mpi         000000000803C01B  Unknown               Unknown  Unknown

Dear Zakaria,
Your /home/zm297 and /home/zm297/rds/hpc-work are separate file systems. First one has only 40GB of space and rds has 1TB.  You can type 'quota' to see your usage. You can move your data to hpc-work and run it from there to fix this.
 
Kind regards,
Greg
RCS Team

Cumulus CPU 200,000 hours * £0.01/CPU hour = £2000 PI is Prof Sir David Klenerman dk10012@cam.ac.uk
and
Research Data Store (RDS) 10TB for 3 years = £162/TB* 10 TB = £1620 PI is Prof Sir David Klenerman dk10012@cam.ac.uk
Regards
Dave
Mr David Pratt (he/him/his)
Senior Support Technician
Telephone Liaison Officier
Yusuf Hamied Department Of Chemistry,
University of Cambridge,
Lensfield Rd. Cambridge, CB2 1EW
Direct Line 01223 747611
Murphy's Law
If everything seems to be going well, you have obviously overlooked something.
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Procurement / Pricing Information
Technical HPC Action Required: No
Finance Action Required: No (information only)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is requesting a formal quote for:
- Cumulus CPU 200,000 hours at £0.01/CPU hour
- Research Data Store (RDS) 10 TB for 3 years at £162/TB

HPC Support does not issue formal quotes. Pricing information is published and departments must raise purchase orders via their own finance processes.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)

1) Clarify that formal quotes are not issued.
2) Confirm the published pricing model (if appropriate).
3) Advise that departmental finance teams should proceed using the published rates.
4) Do not generate bespoke quotation documents.
5) Do not raise purchase orders on behalf of departments.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Subject: Re: Quote request for Cumulus CPU and RDS

Dear Dave,

Thank you for your message.

We do not issue formal quotation documents for Cumulus CPU time or RDS storage. The services are charged according to the published pricing, and your departmental finance team can proceed based on those rates.

For reference:
- Cumulus CPU usage is charged per CPU hour at the published rate.
- RDS storage is charged per TB per year according to the current pricing schedule.

If your department requires confirmation of the published pricing, this is available on the HPC documentation pages.

Please let us know if you have any further questions.

Best regards,  
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not generate unofficial quote documents.
- Do not commit to pricing beyond the published rates.
- Do not raise purchase orders on behalf of users.
- Ensure any pricing referenced aligns with the current published documentation.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Purchasing / Quotes
Technical Action Required: No
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user needs quotes for purchasing compute hours and RDS storage. Previous tickets indicate that quotes can be generated for RDS storage through the self-service portal, and email quotes can be provided for compute hours [1][2][3].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Provide an email quote for the compute hours.
2. Direct the user to the self-service portal for the RDS storage quote.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Dave,

Thank you for your request. Here is the email quote for the compute hours:

200,000 Service Level 2 CPU hours at a unit rate of £0.01, total cost of £2000. Owning PI is Prof. David Klenerman (dk10012).

For the Research Data Store (RDS) quote, please use the self-service portal:
https://selfservice.uis.cam.ac.uk/storage/RDS/

Let me know if you require anything else.

Kind regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure that the quotes provided match the requested specifications exactly.
- Always direct users to the self-service portal for RDS storage quotes when possible [3].

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-33452::chunk::0000
[2] : HPCSSUP-73309::chunk::0001
[3] : HPCSSUP-30884::chunk::0007

user_input:
Dear HPC Team,
Could we add a few more recent CUDA modules to the cluster? At the moment I can load cuda/12.1, but many current DL packages (e.g., recent PyTorch/TensorFlow wheels) target CUDA 12.4 or 12.6. Having cuda/12.4 and cuda/12.6available would greatly improve compatibility and avoid building from source.
If feasible, installing these alongside the existing cuda/12.1 (plus matching cuDNN where applicable) would be ideal. I’m happy to test the new modules once they’re deployed.
Many thanks!
Best regards,
Yijiang Dong
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Software Stack / CUDA Module Availability
Technical Action Required: No (guidance only)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User requests CUDA 12.4/12.6 modules for package compatibility. CUDA 12.4 and 12.6 are not officially supported, and CUDA 12.8.1 is available in the Ampere test stack.

ACTION
1) Clarify that CUDA 12.4 and 12.6 are not currently supported.
2) Provide test stack commands for CUDA 12.8.1.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Dear Yijiang,

We do not currently provide official CUDA 12.4 or 12.6 modules. We do, however, have a newer test software stack that includes CUDA 12.8.1.

Please run:

module purge
module load rhel8/slurm
module load rhel8/global
module use /usr/local/software/spack/csd3/spack-modules/test/ampere-2025-06-01/linux-rocky8-zen3
module load cuda/12.8.1/gcc/kdeps6ab

Please let us know if you run into any issues.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not promise unsupported module versions.
- Distinguish test stack from officially supported stack.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Software / Module Installation
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Yes (RSE Team)
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user has requested the installation of CUDA versions 12.4 and 12.6, along with matching cuDNN versions where applicable. This request aligns with previous requests for newer CUDA versions [1][2][3].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Escalate the request to the RSE team for the installation of CUDA 12.4 and CUDA 12.6, along with matching cuDNN versions [3].
2. Inform the user that their request has been forwarded to the RSE team for further action.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Yijiang,

Thank you for your request. We have forwarded your request for the installation of CUDA 12.4 and CUDA 12.6, along with matching cuDNN versions, to our RSE team. We will keep you updated on the progress.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure that the RSE team is informed about the specific versions and the need for matching cuDNN versions [3].
- Do not attempt to install the modules without RSE team approval and support.

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-99885::chunk::0000
[2] : HPCSSUP-98682::chunk::0000
[3] : HPCSSUP-76188::chunk::0001

[TICKET_SUMMARY] Request for CUDA versions

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
Dear CSD3 Support Team,
I am writing to enquire whether it would be possible to install more recent versions of CUDA on the cluster. At present, the latest version available via module avail is 12.1; however, my research depends on features introduced in later releases (12.4, 12.6, 12.8).
Thank you very much for your support.
Kind regards,
Yuval

[TICKET_SUMMARY] Cuda12.2

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
Hello,
I hope you are well.
I am trying to install and compile a package on csd3 which requires cuda12.2 and I am aware that the cluster only has up to cuda12.1. I am therefore wondering if it is by any chance possible to have newer versions of cuda as a module on the cluster?
Thank you!
Best,
Janice

[TICKET_SUMMARY] Need CUDA 12.2

[CONVERSATION]
<MESSAGE id=0001 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>
Hi Xiaochen,

CUDA/12.1 is available on CSD3, please use the following command to load cuda/12.1.

```
module load cuda/12.1
```
Kind regards,
John Metson

</MESSAGE><MESSAGE id=0002 role=TICKET_CREATOR>
Dear John,
Thanks for replying. Yes, I understand that we have cuda 12.1. However, we are trying to replicate the results and develop on a third-party system for distributed training that only supports cuda 12.2. Could you please add 12.2 to the modules available?
Thank you very much.
Best Wishes,
Xiaochen

</MESSAGE><MESSAGE id=0003 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>
Hi Xiaochen,

I will pass this request onto our RSE team.

Kind regards,

reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Remote Access / TurboVNC
Technical Action Required: No (usage correction)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User launched VNC on a login node and attempted local port forward, causing binding/forwarding issues. Correct method is to run VNC on an interactive GPU node (e.g. `*-r-gpu`) following documented workflow.

ACTION
1) Instruct user to create VNC session on an interactive node, not login node.
2) Share the VNC guide and expected command sequence.
3) Confirm resolution after user retest.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hello Iryna,

Please create your TurboVNC session on an interactive GPU node (`*-r-gpu`) rather than a login node. That should resolve the forwarding issue you’re seeing.

Please follow the documented TurboVNC workflow for interactive GPU nodes, and let us know if you run into further issues.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not encourage running long-lived GUI sessions on login nodes.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Software / TurboVNC
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is encountering an error when trying to establish an SSH tunnel for TurboVNC. The error message indicates that the port 5909 is already in use or cannot be assigned. This is a common issue when the specified port is already occupied by another process [2][4].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Verify if the port 5909 is already in use on the user's local machine.
2. Suggest using a different port number for the SSH tunnel.
3. Provide the user with the correct steps to establish the SSH tunnel and connect to TurboVNC.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Iryna,

Thank you for reaching out. The error message you are seeing indicates that the port 5909 is already in use or cannot be assigned. This is a common issue when the specified port is already occupied by another process.

Please try the following steps:

1. Verify if the port 5909 is already in use on your local machine.
2. If the port is in use, try using a different port number for the SSH tunnel. For example, you can use port 5910 instead of 5909.

To establish the SSH tunnel with a different port, use the following command:
```
ssh -L 5910:localhost:5909 iv285@login-q-3.hpc.cam.ac.uk
```
Then, in TurboVNC, use `localhost:5910` as the VNC Server address.

If you continue to experience issues, please let us know the output of the following command:
```
netstat -tuln | grep 5909
```
This will help us diagnose the problem further.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Always ensure that the port number used for the SSH tunnel is not already in use.
- Follow the documented steps for establishing an SSH tunnel and connecting to TurboVNC [2][4].

Dear support team,
I need to rsync a large dataset from our rcs project (cc234). Doing it from a login node is probably not the way, as I'm being kicked out, I guess because the rsync is too heavy. Is there a dedicated data transfer node I can ssh to, or should I do it via an interactive job?
Thanks,
Yaniv
Sent from Outlook for Android<[https://aka.ms/AAb9ysg](https://aka.ms/AAb9ysg)>
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / RCS Data Transfer
Technical Action Required: No (best-practice guidance)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User is running heavy `rsync` from login node and getting disconnected. Recommended approach is session persistence (screen/tmux) or GUI transfer workflow; follow RCS transfer best practices.

ACTION
1) Recommend using `screen`/`tmux` for long transfer sessions.
2) Optionally direct to supported GUI transfer methods for RCS.
3) Share RCS best-practice guidance.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hello Yaniv,

For long RCS transfers, please use a persistent terminal session (for example `screen`/`tmux`) so the transfer can continue if your SSH session drops.

You can also use a supported GUI transfer method:
https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/storage/rcs/gui.html

Best-practice guidance:
https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/storage/rcs/best-practice.html

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Avoid advising large unsupervised transfers directly from transient login sessions.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / Data Transfer
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
RCS is not mounted on compute nodes, so rsync should be performed from a login node. Using an interactive job is not suitable for RCS transfers [1][2][4].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Advise the user to perform rsync from a login node.
2. Suggest using a screen session to avoid disconnection issues [3].

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Yaniv,

RCS is not mounted on compute nodes, so you should perform the rsync from a login node. To avoid disconnection issues, you can use a screen session. Here is an example command for rsync:

```
rsync -v --progress [source] [destination]
```

For more details, please refer to our documentation: [https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/storage/rds/cli.html](https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/storage/rds/cli.html) [3]

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not advise using compute nodes for RCS transfers [1][2][4].
- Suggest using screen sessions for long-running rsync operations to prevent disconnections [3].

From: Avraam Papadopoulos (Jira) <support@hpc.cam.ac.uk>

Reply above this line.
Avraam Papadopoulos commented:
Dear Natalie,
RCS is not mounted on the CSD3 compute nodes only the login nodes. This means that you can only access it from the login nodes and not from any job that runs in a compute node. Did you have any problems transferring data from RFS to RCS from a login node and that's why you tried to submit a job?
Kind regards,
Avraam

[TICKET_SUMMARY] Rsync errors - RFS to RCS

[CONVERSATION]
<MESSAGE id=0003 role=TICKET_CREATOR>
Hi Avraam, 
  
Thanks for your quick response. 
I tried to submit using a slurm job initially as I thought this was best practice and I’m pretty sure I did this previously and it worked? 
However it isn’t working at the moment. 
  
On the other hand, when I do it directly on command line (I assume this is login node) it works perfectly. However, this takes a while and I am moving a lot of data – another reason why I wanted to submit a slurm job.  
  
Should I just continue to do it manually for every file on command line?
Many thankss 
Natalie 
  
–     
  
   
From: Avraam Papadopoulos (Jira) <support@hpc.cam.ac.uk>
Date:  Thursday, 9 March 2023 at 10:37
To:  Natalie Wallis <njw64@cam.ac.uk>
Subject:  HPCSSUP-54593 Rsync errors - RFS to RCS   
—-—-—-—
Reply above this line.
Avraam Papadopoulos commented:
Dear Natalie,
RCS is not mounted on the CSD3 compute nodes only the login nodes. This means that you can only access it from the login nodes and not from any job that runs in a compute node. Did you have any problems transferring data from RFS to RCS from a login node and that's why you tried to submit a job?
Kind regards,
Avraam

user_input:
Hi,
We are having some issues with various ceph shares on the Arcus cloud 
today and yesterday. Seeing metadata servers switch to reconnecting e.g.:
> a900cf30-f8a3-42bf-98d6-af7ce92f1a1a.client1188705722/mds_sessions
> ::::::::::::::
> global_id 1188705722
> name "iris-gaia-code-spec-ro"
> mds.1 reconnecting
> a900cf30-f8a3-42bf-98d6-af7ce92f1a1a.client1188705717/mds_sessions
> ::::::::::::::
> global_id 1188705717
> name "process-space-1"
> mds.1 reconnecting
This is causing filesystem access to hang.
Rebooting the instances seems to be clearing this in some cases but it 
is happening on multiple instances and also recurring. Is there any 
indication of an underlying issue?
Cheers,
Patrick.
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / CephFS / MDS connectivity (Arcus cloud)
Technical Action Required: Yes (service-side investigation/mitigation)
Escalation Required: Yes (Ceph/storage administrators + network)
Urgency: High (filesystem hangs across multiple instances)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User reports Ceph shares on Arcus cloud hanging across multiple instances, with client session state showing repeated MDS reconnecting behaviour and filesystem access hanging. Because the issue affects multiple shares and instances, this is more consistent with a service-side CephFS/MDS or network-path problem than a single-instance misconfiguration. Rebooting instances may temporarily clear symptoms but should not be treated as the primary fix.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1) Acknowledge and treat as a service incident
   - Confirm the issue affects multiple instances/shares.
   - Prioritise service-side checks over detailed user-side debugging.

2) Collect minimal high-signal diagnostics from the user
   Ask for:
   - example affected instance name(s)
   - approximate timestamps of failures
   - exact mount command(s) or error output if readily available

3) Service-side investigation (storage/admin)
   Check:
   - Ceph cluster health summary and recent warnings
   - CephFS filesystem status and active/standby MDS state
   - MDS daemon logs around the failure window
   - client session reconnect/eviction behaviour
   - any ongoing rebalancing or recovery that could affect metadata availability

4) Network path verification (admin)
   Verify routing/interface configuration and any recent changes affecting CephFS traffic.

5) Ask users to retry only after backend checks or remediation have been completed.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Patrick,

Thanks for the detailed report. Because the hangs are affecting multiple instances and shares, this looks more like a service-side CephFS issue than a single-instance configuration problem.

We’re investigating the backend now. In the meantime, could you please share:
- the names of one or two affected instances,
- the approximate timestamps of the most recent failures,
- and one example of the mount command or error output you are seeing?

Once we have checked the backend state, we can advise whether any instance-side action is still needed.

Cheers,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Treat multi-instance CephFS hangs as a service incident; avoid shifting burden to users.
- Avoid making claims about root cause unless supported by service-side evidence.
- Suggest disruptive workarounds (instance reboot) only with explicit user confirmation and after backend stability is restored.
- Do not request or expose sensitive infrastructure details in user-facing messages.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / Ceph Cluster Issue
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Yes
Urgency: High

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The issue described is similar to previous incidents where Ceph shares on the Arcus cloud were not connecting, causing filesystem access to hang [1][2][3]. This is a recurring problem that affects multiple instances and can be critical for ongoing processing tasks.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Escalate the issue to the relevant technical team for immediate investigation.
2. Inform the user that the issue is being escalated and provide an estimated time for a response.
3. Monitor the situation and provide updates to the user as they become available.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Patrick,

Thank you for reporting this issue. We have escalated the problem to our technical team for immediate investigation. This issue appears to be similar to previous incidents where Ceph shares on the Arcus cloud were not connecting, causing filesystem access to hang [1][2][3].

We will keep you updated on the progress and provide an estimated time for a response as soon as possible.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure that the issue is escalated promptly to avoid further disruption to the user's processing tasks.
- Provide regular updates to the user to keep them informed about the status of the issue.

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-86663::chunk::0000
[2] : HPCSSUP-87130::chunk::0000
[3] : HPCSSUP-47820::chunk::0001

Dear support team,
Our team wanted to top up some more GPU core times and extra rds disk spaces for project 'QAKG-SL2-GPU'? Could you please give us a quote for the price of 10000 extra GPU hours and 20 GB extra rds space?
Best wishes,
Wenyu
The University of Edinburgh is a charitable body, registered in Scotland, with registration number SC005336. Is e buidheann carthannais a th' ann an Oilthigh Dh?n ?ideann, cl?raichte an Alba, ?ireamh cl?raidh SC005336.
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: CORE Procurement / Quote Clarification
Technical Action Required: No (quote preparation and clarification)
Finance Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User is requesting pricing for 10,000 additional GPU hours and additional RDS storage on `QAKG-SL2-GPU`. Because the message says `20 GB`, which is unusually small in this procurement context, the safest response is to confirm the intended storage amount and whether the quote should cover only new storage or also any existing storage renewal/extension before issuing a final figure.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1) Clarify the storage request before quoting
   - Confirm whether `20 GB` is correct or a typo.
   - Confirm whether the user means additional capacity on top of the current licensed storage.
   - Confirm whether the quote should cover only extra storage or also renewal/extension of existing storage.

2) Quote the GPU hours separately using the current applicable rate.

3) Once the storage details are confirmed, issue a written email quote covering both the GPU hours and the corrected storage amount.

4) Ask the user to send the PO to the ticket once ready.

5) Keep any later account-creation request separate from the pricing calculation.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Dear Wenyu,

Before I finalise the storage part of the quote, could you please confirm whether `20 GB` is correct? That is unusually small for this service, so I want to make sure we quote the right storage amount and whether this is additional capacity or an extension of your current storage.

Once you confirm the intended storage quantity and scope, I can provide the full written quote for both storage and GPU hours.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not silently convert user-supplied units.
- If the storage quantity looks implausible, ask before quoting.
- Keep quote assumptions explicit, especially if renewal or extension of existing storage affects cost.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Resource Allocation / Top-Up Request
Technical Action Required: No
Portal Action Required: Yes
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The request involves topping up GPU core hours and RDS disk space for a project. Previous tickets show that top-up requests are handled through purchase orders and involve specific amounts of GPU core hours [1][2][3][4][5].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Inform the user that top-up requests require a purchase order.
2. Direct the user to provide a purchase order for the requested resources.
3. Do not process the request without a valid purchase order.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Wenyu,

To top up GPU core hours and RDS disk space for your project 'QAKG-SL2-GPU', we need a purchase order. Please provide a purchase order for 10,000 extra GPU hours and 20 GB extra RDS space.

Once we receive the purchase order, we will process your request.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not process top-up requests without a valid purchase order [1][2][3][4][5].
- Ensure all resource allocation changes are documented and approved.

Hello,
I am having problems logging in to hpc via ssh irs38@login-gpu.hpc.cam.ac.uk as usual, even though an email has been sent out confirming the end of the maintenance work. I just wanted to check if there isn't something I am missing, or if it is just taking time until hpc will be fully available.
Kind regards,
Ieva
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Service Availability / Post-Maintenance
Technical Action Required: No
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User reports inability to SSH shortly after maintenance completion notice. Correct response is that services may still be coming online; direct users to service status and communications.

ACTION
1) Inform user service restoration is still in progress.
2) Provide status page and advise monitoring updates.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hello Ieva,

We are still bringing services online following maintenance. Please keep an eye on service communications and the status page for updates:
https://www.hpc.cam.ac.uk/service-status

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not declare full service restoration until confirmed.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: HPC / Login Issues
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is experiencing issues logging into the HPC via SSH after maintenance. Similar issues have been reported in the past, often due to partial availability of login nodes [1][2][3][4][5].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Verify the current status of the login nodes, particularly login-gpu.hpc.cam.ac.uk.
2. Check if there are any ongoing maintenance notifications or issues with the login nodes.
3. If the issue persists, escalate to the HPC support team for further investigation.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you provide the exact error message you are receiving when trying to log in?
- Have you tried logging into alternative nodes such as login-e-1.hpc.cam.ac.uk, login-e-2.hpc.cam.ac.uk, etc.?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Ieva,

Thank you for your message. We are aware of recent maintenance work on the HPC system. There might be some ongoing issues with the login nodes, particularly login-gpu.hpc.cam.ac.uk.

Please try logging into one of the following alternative nodes:
- login-e-1.hpc.cam.ac.uk
- login-e-2.hpc.cam.ac.uk
- login-e-3.hpc.cam.ac.uk
- login-e-4.hpc.cam.ac.uk

If you continue to experience issues, please provide the exact error message you are receiving so we can assist you further.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure that the user is informed about any ongoing maintenance or issues with the login nodes.
- Do not provide login credentials or sensitive information in the response.

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-62356::chunk::0000
[2] : HPCSSUP-19730::chunk::0003
[3] : HPCSSUP-66727::chunk::0001
[4] : HPCSSUP-92106::chunk::0000
[5] : HPCSSUP-71354::chunk::0001

user_input:
Hi HPC Support Team,
I noticed that my home directory does not have a symlink ~/rfs pointing to the RFS project folder. Currently, I can access the project data using the absolute path /rfs/project/rfs-iCNyzSAaucw, but having the symlink would make it much more convenient.
Could you please create the ~/rfs symlink in my home directory?
Thank you so much for your help!
Best regards,
Hansheng
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Filesystem / Symlink / Home Quota
Technical Action Required: No (user guidance only)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User wants a convenience symlink from `~/rfs` to `/rfs/project/rfs-iCNyzSAaucw`. The correct command is straightforward, but the ticket shows the attempt failed because the user's `/home` directory was already over quota. In that case the symlink itself cannot be created until home usage is reduced.

ACTION
1) Ask the user to create the link with the concrete command:
   - `cd $HOME`
   - `ln -s /rfs/project/rfs-iCNyzSAaucw $HOME/rfs`
2) If they see `No space left on device`, ask for `quota` output.
3) Explain that the immediate problem is `/home` usage above the 50 GB quota, not an RFS permission problem.
4) Advise them to remove unneeded files from `/home` or move suitable data to `hpc-work`.
5) Mention that accidentally deleted files from `/home` may be recoverable from `~/.snapshot`.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hello Hansheng,

Please try:

  cd $HOME
  ln -s /rfs/project/rfs-iCNyzSAaucw $HOME/rfs

If you receive `No space left on device`, please run `quota` and send us the output. That would usually indicate your `/home` directory is full, which would prevent creation of even a small symlink entry.

If your home usage is over quota, you will need to move some data into `hpc-work` or delete unwanted files from `/home` before retrying. If you remove anything from `/home` by mistake, you can usually restore it from `~/.snapshot`.

Best wishes,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Use the concrete path from the user request when it is already known.
- Distinguish `/home` quota exhaustion from RFS access problems.
- Do not advise deleting RFS data to fix a `/home` quota issue.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / Symlink Creation
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user needs a symlink to their RFS project folder in their home directory. This is a common request and can be handled by providing the user with the necessary commands to create the symlink themselves [1][2][3][4].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
- Provide the user with the commands to create the symlink.
- Ask the user to confirm if the symlink creation was successful.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Hansheng,

You can create a symlink to your RFS project folder in your home directory using the following commands:

```
cd
mkdir rfs
cd rfs
ln -s /rfs/project/rfs-iCNyzSAaucw rfs-iCNyzSAaucw
```

Please try these commands and let us know if you encounter any issues.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure the user has the correct permissions to create symlinks in their home directory.
- Verify the provided path is correct and accessible to the user.

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-63266::chunk::0002
[2] : HPCSSUP-89742::chunk::0002
[3] : HPCSSUP-89614::chunk::0001
[4] : HPCSSUP-89207::chunk::0002

user_input:
hello,
after upgrading from gfortran-5.4 to gfortran-13.3 my fortran based mpi 
code produced interface related warnings during compile and crashed 
during execution. Replacing 'include mpif.h' statements with 'use mpi' 
statements (plus a few other changes) fixed the problems on my local 
linux box. However, when compiling on CSD3 I get the following mpi.mod 
error:
f951: Fatal Error: Reading module 
'/usr/local/software/spack/csd3/opt-2024-06-01/linux-rocky8-cascadelake/gcc-13.3.0/intel-oneapi-mpi-2021.12.1-cvatknvvaxe4ohzbsri6keicyjoy3w7f/mpi/2021.12/include/mpi/mpi.mod' 
at line 1 column 2: Unexpected EOF
The code is in /home/mh526/Atham/svn/branches/FlAshPlume. The compiler 
settings are in 
/home/mh526/Atham/svn/branches/FlAshPlume/source/Makefile.COMPILE
For the compile I load the following modules:
module load rhel8/cclake/base
module load gcc/13.3.0/7ukda7ns
module load netcdf-fortran-pnetcdf/4.6.1/gcc/intel-oneapi-mpi/ptuvn62k
The compile command  that produces the error is
/usr/local/software/spack/csd3/opt-2024-06-01/linux-rocky8-cascadelake/gcc-13.3.0/intel-oneapi-mpi-2021.12.1-cvatknvvaxe4ohzbsri6keicyjoy3w7f/mpi/2021.12/bin/mpif90 
-c -DMPI -g -O3 -ffree-line-length-none -mcmodel=medium 
-I/usr/local/software/spack/csd3/opt-2024-06-01/linux-rocky8-cascadelake/gcc-13.3.0/netcdf-fortran-4.6.1-ptuvn62k6et3sihhin2vrwbtyjuycp4o/include 
-I/usr/local/software/spack/csd3/opt-2024-06-01/linux-rocky8-cascadelake/gcc-13.3.0/parallel-netcdf-1.12.3-sn2y3yw32wdavsrvyy6g2dgru3x363d3/include 
/home/mh526/Atham/svn/branches/FlAshPlume/source/Basic/precision.F90
Are you able to tell me what the problem is? Am I using the wrong 
mpi.mod file?
Many thanks,
Michael
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Build/Compiler Toolchain / MPI Fortran Modules
Technical Action Required: Yes (workaround instruction)
Escalation Required: Optional (packaging improvement)
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
`gfortran 13.3` with Intel MPI hit `mpi.mod` read error (`Unexpected EOF`) because Intel MPI Fortran module files in that build were available only for older gfortran ABI versions. Workaround: explicitly include compatible `gfortran/11.1.0` (or `10.2.0`) MPI module path.

ACTION
1) Confirm exact module environment and minimal repro.
2) Explain ABI/module version mismatch.
3) Provide compile workaround with explicit include path.
4) Confirm successful compile with user.
5) Track packaging improvement for future stack.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hi Michael,

Please try compiling with an explicit MPI Fortran module include path:

mpif90 -I /usr/local/software/spack/csd3/opt-2024-06-01/linux-rocky8-cascadelake/gcc-13.3.0/intel-oneapi-mpi-2021.12.1-cvatknvvaxe4ohzbsri6keicyjoy3w7f/mpi/2021.12/include/mpi/gfortran/11.1.0 -c -DMPI precision.F90

(Using `.../gfortran/10.2.0` is also a valid workaround.)

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Be explicit this is a compatibility workaround, not ideal long-term packaging.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Compilation / MPI Module Issue
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is experiencing a compilation error related to the MPI module when upgrading from gfortran-5.4 to gfortran-13.3. The error indicates an issue with the `mpi.mod` file, which is not uncommon when there is a mismatch between the compiler and the MPI library versions [1][2][3].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Verify the compatibility of the loaded modules with the gfortran-13.3 compiler.
2. Check if there is a known issue with the specific version of the Intel OneAPI MPI library being used.
3. If necessary, escalate to the HPC support team for further investigation.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you provide the exact error message when attempting to compile with the new modules?
- Have you tried purging all modules and reloading them in a specific order?
- Are there any specific compiler flags or settings that might be causing this issue?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Michael,

Thank you for your detailed report. The issue you're experiencing with the `mpi.mod` file is likely due to a mismatch between the compiler and the MPI library versions [1][2][3].

Please try the following steps:
1. Purge all currently loaded modules using `module purge`.
2. Reload the necessary modules in the following order:
   ```
   module load rhel8/cclake/base
   module load gcc/13.3.0/7ukda7ns
   module load netcdf-fortran-pnetcdf/4.6.1/gcc/intel-oneapi-mpi/ptuvn62k
   ```
3. Attempt to compile your code again.

If the issue persists, please provide the exact error message and any additional details that might help us diagnose the problem further.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure that the modules loaded are compatible with the compiler version being used.
- Avoid mixing modules from different compiler versions unless explicitly documented as compatible.

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-22110::chunk::0002
[2] : HPCSSUP-10400::chunk::0000
[3] : HPCSSUP-82204::chunk::0000

reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Job Execution / MPI under Slurm
Technical Action Required: No (user guidance with documented launch pattern)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User attempted direct `mpirun` and saw MPI startup/initialisation errors. For this environment, MPI jobs should be run inside a Slurm allocation using the documented submission pattern. Running directly from a login shell is a common cause of PMPI/launcher errors.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1) Confirm launch context
   - Ask whether command was run:
     - directly on a login node, or
     - inside a Slurm job allocation.

2) Provide documented MPI job pattern
   - Instruct user to submit via `sbatch` with account/partition/resources set.
   - Recommend launching tasks with `srun` inside the job script.

3) Provide minimal working template
```bash
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH -A <ACCOUNT>
#SBATCH -p <PARTITION>
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --ntasks=1
#SBATCH --time=00:10:00

module purge
module load <required-modules>

srun ~/meme-mpi/bin/meme --help
```

4) If user needs interactive testing
   - First allocate resources interactively (documented method), then run with `srun` in that allocation.

5) Reference documentation
   - Share the MPI jobs section for the relevant partition.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Dear Milena,

This error usually occurs when MPI is launched outside a proper Slurm allocation.

Please run your MPI command through a Slurm job script, for example:

```bash
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH -A <ACCOUNT>
#SBATCH -p <PARTITION>
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --ntasks=1
#SBATCH --time=00:10:00

module purge
module load <required-modules>

srun ~/meme-mpi/bin/meme --help
```

Submit with:

`sbatch <script_name>.sh`

Please also refer to:
https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/user-guide/a100.html?highlight=mpirun#jobs-requiring-mpi

Using the documented Slurm launcher pattern should resolve this startup issue.

Regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Avoid ad-hoc launcher advice that bypasses Slurm resource allocation.
- Keep examples aligned with documented partition/account usage.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Software / MPI
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is encountering an error when trying to run an MPI-enabled process on the nodes. The error message indicates a problem with the MPI initialization, which is a common issue reported in several tickets [1][2][3][4]. The exact cause of the error may vary, but it often relates to the MPI environment or configuration.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Verify the user's MPI environment and configuration.
2. Check if the user has loaded the correct modules for MPI.
3. Review the submission script and ensure it is correctly set up for MPI jobs.
4. If the issue persists, escalate to a higher-level support team for further investigation.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you provide the full submission script you are using?
- Have you tried running the job on different nodes (e.g., cclake nodes) to see if the issue persists?
- What modules are currently loaded in your environment? (You can check this with the `module list` command.)

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Milena,

Thank you for your query. The error you are encountering is related to the MPI initialization process. This is a common issue that has been reported in several tickets [1][2][3][4].

To help us diagnose the problem, could you please provide the full submission script you are using? Additionally, have you tried running the job on different nodes (e.g., cclake nodes) to see if the issue persists?

It would also be helpful to know what modules are currently loaded in your environment. You can check this with the `module list` command.

Please get back in touch with this information, and we will assist you further.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure that the user's MPI environment is correctly configured before running MPI jobs.
- Escalate to higher-level support if the issue cannot be resolved with basic troubleshooting steps.

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
Dear HPC Support,
I have a code (using MPI) that previously run on the Skylake nodes. I have been trying to get it to run on the Icelake nodes but so far have no luck. I didn’t load any extra modules except the default with these lines in the submission script:
module purge
module load rhel8/default-icl   # REQUIRED - loads the basic environment
The code appears to compile alright but it won’t run and produce the following error messages:
----------
MPI startup(): Warning: I_MPI_PMI_LIBRARY will be ignored since the hydra process manager was found
MPI startup(): Warning: I_MPI_PMI_LIBRARY will be ignored since the hydra process manager was found

user_input:
Hi,
I have noticed that me and my colleague are unable to connect to the RCS. We both are connecting by SFTP, rcs.uis.cam.ac.uk on port 22 and both get the same unable to connect message.
We have also had a ticket from a user in our department explaining that she is also unable to connect where she was able to last week.
Is there a problem with the service at the moment? Or has anything changed that might be causing this?
Thanks
Kind Regards,
Shaun Cooke (he/him)
IT Service Manager
Department of Plant Sciences
University of Cambridge
Downing Street, Cambridge, CB2 3EA
Tel: 44 (0)1223 748962 | stc48@cam.ac.uk<[stc48@cam.ac.uk](mailto:stc48@cam.ac.uk)>
[https://www.plantsci.cam.ac.uk<https://www.plantsci.cam.ac.uk/](https://www.plantsci.cam.ac.uk%3Chttps://www.plantsci.cam.ac.uk/)>
This email (together with any files transmitted with it) is intended only for the use of the individual(s) to whom it is addressed. It may contain information which is confidential and/or legally privileged. If you have received this email in error, please notify the sender and delete the original message.
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Access / RCS Connectivity / SSH-MFA
Technical Action Required: No (user onboarding guidance)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User reports that multiple people are unable to connect to `rcs.uis.cam.ac.uk` over SFTP/SSH, raising the possibility of either a broader service problem or account-specific access changes. The correct first response is to confirm whether there is an active service incident and, if not, gather examples from affected users to determine whether the problem is shared or user-specific.

ACTION
1) Check whether there is an active service issue affecting RCS SSH/SFTP access.
2) Ask for example usernames, client/method used, and the exact error messages seen by affected users.
3) If the service is healthy, distinguish between users who cannot access RCS at all and users with account-specific denial errors.
4) For individual users, confirm SSH/storage MFA enrolment if relevant and ask them to raise their own tickets for account-specific checks.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Shaun,

We’re first checking whether there is any broader issue affecting RCS SSH/SFTP access.

If the service itself is healthy, we’ll need the affected usernames, the client or method being used, and the exact error messages so we can tell whether this is a shared problem or an account-specific one.

For users who can access the web portal but not SSH/SFTP services, we may also need to confirm that SSH/storage MFA has been set up separately.

If any individual user continues to have problems, please ask them to raise their own ticket so we can check their account details directly.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not troubleshoot third-party user accounts through one reporter without user-specific consent/context.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / RCS Connectivity
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
Multiple users are experiencing issues connecting to the RCS via SFTP, indicating a potential widespread issue. Similar issues have been reported in the past [1][2][3].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Check the status of the RCS service and any ongoing maintenance or issues.
2. Verify if there are any recent changes to the fs-sftp-gw3 server or related infrastructure [4].
3. If the issue persists, escalate to the relevant technical team for further investigation.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you provide the exact error message you are receiving when trying to connect?
- Are you able to connect to other services or is the issue specific to RCS?
- Have there been any recent changes to your network configuration or firewall settings?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Shaun,

Thank you for your message. We are aware of similar issues reported by other users [1][2][3]. Our team is currently investigating the matter.

In the meantime, could you please provide the exact error message you are receiving when trying to connect? This will help us diagnose the issue more accurately.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure that any changes to the infrastructure are properly documented and communicated to users.
- Follow the standard escalation procedure if the issue cannot be resolved promptly.

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-29314::chunk::0000
[2] : HPCSSUP-89893::chunk::0000
[3] : HPCSSUP-91273::chunk::0000
[4] : HPCSSUP-88524::chunk::0001

Good morning, I am doing a benchmarking exercise on the ukaea-spr and ukaea-spr-hbm nodes.
I've managed to find most of the hardware info I need with some probing,
but for completeness I could do with some information as follows:
Network Interconnect
Network Topology
Storage Device Type (on the RDS filesystem) i.e. SSD/HDD/NVMe
Many Thanks in Advance,
Aleks
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: System Information / Hardware Specs
Technical Action Required: No
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User requested platform-level hardware details for benchmarking. Provided high-level interconnect/topology/storage architecture info.

ACTION
Provide requested specs:
- Interconnect: one Infiniband NDR 200 per node
- Topology: fat-tree
- Storage type: Lustre filesystem

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Dear Aleks,

For `ukaea-spr-hbm`, the requested details are:
- Network interconnect: one Infiniband NDR 200 per node
- Network topology: fat tree
- Storage type (RDS): Lustre filesystem

Please let us know if you need anything further.

Best wishes,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Keep responses at approved/public detail level.
response:

Category: Hardware / Benchmarking
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium

user_input:
Hi,
I have been able to log onto the VPN but when I try to login to Citrix Workspace I just get the error message attached. Are you able to help?
Best,
Dan
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: SRCP Access / Citrix Client Troubleshooting
Technical Action Required: No (diagnostic guidance)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User on VPN is unable to log in via the Citrix Workspace app. The appropriate first diagnostic is to verify whether browser-based launch through `anywhere.cam.ac.uk` works and whether the SRCP desktop can be started there, which helps separate a Citrix client problem from a broader access issue.

ACTION
1) Ask the user to test web launch via `https://anywhere.cam.ac.uk/`.
2) Verify whether the SRCP desktop is visible there and whether launching from the browser hands off to Citrix correctly.
3) If browser launch works, focus on the local Citrix Workspace client configuration or version.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Dear Daniel,

While connected to VPN, please log in via:
https://anywhere.cam.ac.uk/

Do you see your SRCP virtual desktop there, and does launching from the browser open Citrix correctly?

Regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not assume Citrix backend fault without basic path validation.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Citrix Workspace / Login Issues
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is experiencing issues logging into Citrix Workspace despite being connected to the VPN. This is a common issue that can often be resolved by resetting the Citrix program or providing more information about the error and the user's setup.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Direct the user to reset the Citrix program by following the instructions at https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/srcp/swd/faq.html [2].
2. If the reset does not resolve the issue, ask the user to provide screenshots of any error messages they see [2].
3. Request additional information such as the user's operating system, how they are using Citrix, and whether they are on the Cambridge VPN [3].
4. Ask the user to visit https://anywhere.cam.ac.uk/ in an internet browser and report what they see [3].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- What operating system are you using?
- How are you using Citrix (e.g., through a browser or the Citrix Workspace app)?
- Can you provide a screenshot of the error message you are seeing?
- Can you visit https://anywhere.cam.ac.uk/ in an internet browser and let us know what you see?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Dan,

Thank you for reaching out. To resolve the Citrix Workspace login issue, please follow these steps:

1. Reset the Citrix program by following the instructions at https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/srcp/swd/faq.html [2].
2. If the reset does not resolve the issue, please provide screenshots of any error messages you see [2].
3. Additionally, please let us know your operating system and how you are using Citrix (e.g., through a browser or the Citrix Workspace app) [3].
4. Finally, please visit https://anywhere.cam.ac.uk/ in an internet browser and let us know what you see [3].

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure that the user is connected to the Cambridge VPN before attempting to log into Citrix Workspace [1][2][3][4].
- If the issue persists, escalate to the SRCP team for further investigation [4].

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-94787::chunk::0002
[2] : HPCSSUP-99950::chunk::0001
[3] : HPCSSUP-86599::chunk::0001
[4] : HPCSSUP-80200::chunk::0002

user_input:
Dear Support Team,
I've recently added another DiRAC project (DP371) to my user account (rab200) via SAFE, but the resources are not showing up as available on CSD3/Wilkes. 'mybalance' doesn't show the project. I think the account code is probably "DIRAC-DP371-GPU" but I submissions with that account fail. Could help with this?
Cheers,
Richard
==========================================================
Dr. Richard Booth
Royal Society University Research Fellow & University Academic Fellow
School of Physics & Astronomy
W. H. Bragg Building
University of Leeds, UK
Email: r.a.booth@leeds.ac.uk<[r.a.booth@leeds.ac.uk](mailto:r.a.booth@leeds.ac.uk)>
My working hours may differ from yours
I do not expect a reply outside of your own working hours
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Account Provisioning / Project Membership
Technical Action Required: Yes (provisioning completion)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User added a DiRAC project in SAFE but it is not yet visible in `mybalance`. Provisioning is not immediate, so the next step is to check whether the account association is still pending and complete the internal provisioning workflow if required.

ACTION
1) Check provisioning status for requested project association.
2) Complete pending provisioning.
3) Once provisioning has completed, confirm that the account is visible and usable.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hello Richard,

Provisioning for this project is not automatic. We will check whether the project association is still pending on our side and complete the required provisioning step if necessary.

We will update you once that has been checked.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not treat SAFE change as instantly reflected in Slurm/accounting systems.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / Project Management
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user has added a new DiRAC project (DP371) via SAFE but it is not showing up in CSD3/Wilkes. The user suspects the account code is "DIRAC-DP371-GPU" but submissions with that account fail. Similar issues have been reported in previous tickets where projects added via SAFE do not immediately reflect on the cluster [1][2][3].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Verify the project DP371 in SAFE and ensure it is correctly linked to the user's account.
2. Check if the project has been provisioned on CSD3/Wilkes.
3. If the project is correctly provisioned, verify the account code "DIRAC-DP371-GPU" and ensure it is valid.
4. If the project is not provisioned, escalate to the appropriate team for manual provisioning.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you confirm the exact project code and account code as listed in SAFE?
- Have you received any confirmation from SAFE that the project has been fully set up?
- Can you provide the output of the `mybalance` command?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Richard,

Thank you for your message. We will need to verify the status of your project DP371 in SAFE and ensure it is correctly provisioned on CSD3/Wilkes. This process may take some time as it involves manual steps.

Please confirm the exact project code and account code as listed in SAFE. Additionally, if you have received any confirmation from SAFE that the project has been fully set up, please let us know.

We will keep you updated on the progress.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure that all manual provisioning steps are documented and followed as per the standard procedures [1][2][3].
- Do not guess account codes without verified evidence.

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-46688::chunk::0000
[2] : HPCSSUP-21241::chunk::0000
[3] : HPCSSUP-30805::chunk::0011

user_input:
Dear HPC Team,
I am currently using the DAWN cluster and would like to leverage the Intel GPUs for running my PyTorch code.
According to the documentation, the following setup works well:
module load intelpython-conda
conda activate pytorch-gpu
And in Python:
import torch
import intel_extension_for_pytorch as ipex
...
1. Enable GPU
model = model.to('xpu')
data = data.to('xpu')
model = ipex.optimize(model, dtype=torch.float32)
This setup functions as expected.
However, the pytorch-gpu environment appears to be a system-provided environment. For the purposes of my project, I would like to create and manage my own local Conda environment that is compatible with the Intel GPUs (i.e., includes PyTorch and the Intel Extension for PyTorch with xpu support).
I have attempted to clone the pytorch-gpu environment as well as install all of its modules manually, but I run into repeated HTTP 403 errors when trying to fetch packages from the Intel Conda channel. This seems to indicate restricted access or deprecation of some packages or channels.
Could you please advise on the recommended way to:
1.  Create a local Conda environment compatible with the Intel GPU setup used on DAWN,
2.  Ensure installation of all necessary packages without running into access issues?
If there is a supported method to export or replicate the pytorch-gpu environment into a user-managed Conda environment, that would be ideal.
Thank you very much for your assistance!
Best wishes,
Moritz
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Software Environment / Dawn PVC / PyTorch-IPEX
Technical Action Required: No (user setup guidance)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User wants a modifiable local environment equivalent to centrally managed `pytorch-gpu`. Central env is immutable. Supported options:
- activate central `pytorch-gpu` then layer user virtualenv, or
- build user conda env with compatible pinned versions from Intel channel and test on PVC nodes.

ACTION
1) Confirm central env baseline works.
2) Explain central env cannot be modified.
3) Provide two supported approaches:
   - `pytorch-gpu` + user virtualenv layering
   - standalone conda env with compatible pins (`intel-extension-for-pytorch`, `pytorch`, python range) and Intel repo
4) Require testing on PVC/PVC9 interactive node.
5) Set required env vars for XPU runtime test.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hi Moritz,

`pytorch-gpu` is centrally managed and cannot be modified directly.

Supported options are:
1) Activate `pytorch-gpu`, then activate your own virtualenv for extra packages.
2) Create your own conda env with compatible versions (e.g. pinned `intel-extension-for-pytorch` + `pytorch`) and test it on PVC nodes.

Please use the Dawn guidance here:
https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/user-guide/pvc.html#machine-learning-data-science-frameworks

Best wishes,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not advise modifying centrally managed environments.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Software / Environment Management
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is attempting to create a local Conda environment compatible with Intel GPUs on DAWN but is encountering HTTP 403 errors when trying to fetch packages from the Intel Conda channel. This indicates restricted access or deprecation of some packages or channels. The user has tried cloning the pytorch-gpu environment and installing packages manually without success [1][2].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Verify the availability and accessibility of the Intel Conda channel and packages.
2. Check if there are any known issues or updates regarding the Intel Conda channel.
3. Provide the user with a step-by-step guide to create a local Conda environment compatible with Intel GPUs on DAWN.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you provide the exact error messages you are encountering when trying to fetch packages from the Intel Conda channel?
- Have you tried using a different Conda channel or package source?
- Are there any specific packages or versions you need for your project?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Moritz,

reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage Path Discovery
Technical Action Required: No
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User could not locate AIRR/Dawn storage path in docs. The exact project path is user/project-specific and should be discovered from the user account rather than assumed.

ACTION
1) Ask user to check available RDS links under home:
   - `ls -ld ~/rds/*`
2) Ask user to identify the AIRR project path from quota/project listing:
   - `quota`
3) Confirm the matching path format once identified (typically `~/rds/rds-<project-id>` symlink).
4) Clarify that `~/rds/...` is a convenience link to the underlying project path.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Dear Phil,

Your AIRR storage path is user/project specific. Please run:

`ls -ld ~/rds/*`
`quota`

This will show the project link available in your account (typically `~/rds/rds-<project-id>`).  
That entry is a symlink to the underlying project storage location.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not hardcode project-specific paths unless verified for that user/account.

user_input:
Hi -
I’ve just sbatched a job on the HPC, using cclake, job 9082481_[1-2]. If I squeue -u has27 it tells me (under NODELIST) 'ReqNodeNotAvail, Reserved for maintenance’. But maintenance finished last week.
Can you tell me what (if anything) I’ve done wrong? Script below.
Thanks,
Hamish
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH -A GLOVER-SL3-CPU
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --mail-type=NONE
#SBATCH -p cclake  # name of the partition to run job on
#SBATCH -o /home/has27/rds/hpc-work/f2reads/bowtie2/bowtie2%a.log
#SBATCH -c 8         # number of CPUs. Default: 1
#SBATCH --mem=27360M     # RAM memory.
#SBATCH -t 12:00:00  # time for the job HH:MM:SS. Default: 1 min
#SBATCH -a 1-2
source ~/.bashrc
1. activate conda environment
conda activate bowtie2
cd /home/has27/rds/hpc-work/f2reads/soapnuke/clean
1. List all files in the folder starting with A or B,
2. then get the relevant line
INDIV_ID=$(ls -d [AB]* | head -n ${SLURM_ARRAY_TASK_ID} | tail -n 1)
cd $INDIV_ID
READ1=$(ls *1.fq.gz)
READ2=$(ls *2.fq.gz)
cd /home/has27/rds/hpc-work/Genomes/LSRagtag
bowtie2 -p 8 -x LSRagtag -1 /home/has27/rds/hpc-work/f2reads/soapnuke/clean/$INDIV_ID/$READ1 -2 /home/has27/rds/hpc-work/f2reads/soapnuke/clean/$INDIV_ID/$READ2 | samtools sort > /home/has27/rds/hpc-work/f2reads/align-LSRagtag/$INDIV_ID.sorted.bam
—
Dr Hamish Symington
Junior Research Fellow, Queens’ College, Cambridge
Academic Skills Tutor and Bye-Fellow, Downing College, Cambridge
Postdoctoral Researcher in the Glover Lab, Department of Plant Sciences, University of Cambridge
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Slurm Scheduling Status Interpretation
Technical Action Required: No (explanation + minor script advice)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User saw scheduler reason text and assumed there was a post-maintenance fault. From the script provided, there is no obvious submission syntax problem, and this kind of reason string can still appear while a job is simply pending under normal scheduler priority. Additional optimisation: explicit `--mem` is redundant for cclake in this case.

ACTION
1) Check live job state and reason (`squeue`, `scontrol`).
2) Explain pending-by-priority behavior.
3) Suggest removing redundant `--mem` directive when CPU request already implies desired memory.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Dear Hamish,

From the script you sent, the job appears to have submitted correctly and may simply be pending under normal scheduler priority.

For this script, `--mem=27360M` is redundant if you already request the corresponding CPU count on `cclake`; removing it can simplify scheduling requests.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Distinguish temporary reason strings from real submission errors.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: HPC / Job Scheduling
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user's job is showing "ReqNodeNotAvail, Reserved for maintenance" despite maintenance being reported as finished. This issue has been reported in similar cases [1][2][3][4][5].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Verify the current status of the cclake partition and any ongoing maintenance.
2. Check if there are any nodes available in the cclake partition that are not reserved for maintenance.
3. If nodes are available, attempt to reschedule the job manually.
4. If no nodes are available, escalate to the HPC maintenance team for further investigation.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you confirm if there are any ongoing maintenance activities that might affect the cclake partition?
- Have you tried resubmitting the job after the reported maintenance period?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Hamish,

Thank you for your message. The issue you're experiencing with the job status "ReqNodeNotAvail, Reserved for maintenance" has been reported in similar cases [1][2][3][4][5]. We will verify the current status of the cclake partition and any ongoing maintenance.

Please confirm if there are any ongoing maintenance activities that might affect the cclake partition. Additionally, have you tried resubmitting the job after the reported maintenance period?

We will get back to you with further updates.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure that any manual rescheduling of jobs is done with caution and only after verifying the availability of nodes.
- Escalate to the HPC maintenance team if the issue persists and no nodes are available.

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-97398::chunk::0000
[2] : HPCSSUP-45849::chunk::0000
[3] : HPCSSUP-85715::chunk::0000
[4] : HPCSSUP-45425::chunk::0000
[5] : HPCSSUP-100328::chunk::0000

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]

I sent a slurm script as an sbatch job yesterday, and when I checked the status this morning it says the following:
JOBID PARTITION     NAME     USER ST          START_TIME  NODES SCHEDNODES           NODELIST(REASON)
20494612    cclake B_Mendel    tm621 PD                 N/A      1 (null)               (ReqNodeNotAvail, Reserved for maintenance)
I am not sure why it is only listing the one node? Please can you let me know when my jobs will run?
Many thanks
Toqir

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
Dear support, 
  
I haven’t used the Cambridge HPC in a while, so perhaps I am missing something. I just submitted a job to icelake and checked squeue, and this is what I see: 
  
    JOBID PARTITION     NAME      USER ST       TIME  NODES NODELIST(REASON) 
          62845551   icelake extracti    ad923 PD       0:00       1 (ReqNodeNotAvail, Reserved for maintenance) 
  
I have never seen this before: “ReqNodeNotAvail, Reserved for maintenance”. Yet, the script I am running is not different from what I have previously run on skylake, actually identical, I just changed skylake to cclake. 
  
Is this normal? 
  
Cheers, 
Andrea

Andrea Degasperi, M.Sc., Ph.D.

[INITIAL_DESCRIPTION]
Hi!
Platform ID: sgqgxl7mc2z
I submitted a job to the SRCP which is stuck in the queue with the message 'ReqNodeNotAvail, Reserved for maintenance' (photo below).
I did an sinfo check and this seems to be slurm-compute-22 which has the 'plnd' tag (photo below). This also seems to be the case for the slurm-gpu-[0-2]. We weren't advised of any maintenance on SRCP nodes. Could you please look into this?

user_input:
Dear sir/madam,
I have been told to contact this email to ask for an MFA because I see the error: ‘When performing ssh to multi.hpc.cam.ac.uk the QR code is replaced either by garbage or is broken up by blank lines, making it unusable.’.
Thank you very much for sorting.
Many thanks,
James
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: MFA Enrollment / Client Rendering Issue
Technical Action Required: No
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
Mac terminal rendering can distort SSH QR code during MFA enrollment. Enrollment can still proceed by scanning if possible, or manually entering the secret key in authenticator app.

ACTION
1) Confirm issue is known for some Mac terminal setups.
2) Provide manual secret-key fallback method.
3) Clarify expected token labels for SSH vs web.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hi James,

This is a known issue on some Mac setups where the QR may render poorly. You can still complete setup by manually entering the secret key in your authenticator app if scanning fails.

Please follow:
https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/user-guide/mfa.html

The SSH token should appear as `CSD3: SSH Login` (distinct from login-web token naming).

Best wishes,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not reset MFA unnecessarily when manual enrollment path is available.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Authentication / MFA
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is experiencing issues with the MFA QR code during SSH login to multi.hpc.cam.ac.uk. This is a known issue and requires resetting the MFA secret [1][2][4].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Reset the MFA secret for the user.
2. Provide the user with instructions on how to set up the MFA again, including the correct SSH command and documentation links [4].

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello James,

Thank you for contacting HPC Support. We have reset your MFA secret. Please follow these steps to set up your MFA again:

1. Open your terminal and enter the following command:
   ```
   ssh <your_username>@multi.hpc.cam.ac.uk
   ```
   Replace `<your_username>` with your actual username.

2. You will be prompted for your University of Cambridge password. After entering your password, a QR code will appear.

3. Scan the QR code with your authenticator app. If the QR code is not recognized, you can manually enter the key provided.

For detailed instructions, please refer to our documentation:
- [Setting up MFA](https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/user-guide/mfa.html)
- [Video instructions](https://www.hpc.cam.ac.uk/getting-started-csd3-tutorials)

If you encounter any further issues, please let us know.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure the user is aware of the correct SSH command and documentation for setting up MFA [4].
- If the user is on a Mac, advise them to set `export LANG=en_GB.UTF-8` in their terminal before performing the SSH to prevent QR code corruption [4].

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-54226::chunk::0000
[2] : HPCSSUP-94321::chunk::0000
[4] : HPCSSUP-88672::chunk::0004

<MESSAGE id=0010 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>
Hello Paul,

- If your reset was for SSH login: then please put the following into your terminal
```
ssh wpb22@multi.hpc.cam.ac.uk
```

Hi Support Team,
I’m working on some SRCNet related projects and am attempting to run a containerised workflow on Azimuth which requires both CUDA and MPI support. I have built the required software built into an Ubuntu container with specific CUDA and MPI versions, but these of course need to be compatible with what is possible to deploy on the instance itself.
Is there a guide to how to set these up correctly? Or some experience with running GPU and MPI codes on Azimuth that I can leverage so that we can progress the SRC work?
I’m happy to provide the Dockerfile so that you can see how things are being built, and information about the code I’m trying to run, if useful.
Kind regards,
Bradley Meyers
